Title: Two glycosyltransferases involved in anthocyanin modification delineated by transcriptome independent component analysis in Arabidopsis thaliana

Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptome ICA/coexpression; Ds transposon knockout mutants (ugt79b1-1, ugt79b1-2); RT-PCR loss of transcript; anthocyanin profiling by HPLC/PDA/ESI-MS; complementation with 35S:UGT79B1; recombinant His-UGT79B1 enzyme assay in E. coli with substrate specificity testing
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 UDP-glycosyltransferase; UGT79 family; PSPG box-containing plant secondary product glycosyltransferase
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers xylose from UDP-xylose to the 2''-hydroxyl of the glucose moiety of cyanidin 3-O-glucoside, forming cyanidin 3-O-xylosyl(1→2)glucoside
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pigmentation and stable anthocyanin accumulation in plants
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin accumulation and formation of xylosylated anthocyanins; knockout drastically reduces total anthocyanin and abolishes major modified anthocyanins
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for downstream modification and stable accumulation of target product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key downstream modification step important for stable anthocyanin accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Coexpressed with anthocyanin biosynthetic genes; clustered with anthocyanin modification genes
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced under anthocyanin-accumulating condition (12% sucrose); similar expression profile to anthocyanin-specific biosynthetic genes
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Growth on 12% sucrose-containing medium to induce anthocyanin accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray transcriptome coexpression and independent component analysis using 1388 Affymetrix ATH1 arrays from ATTED-II/AtGenExpress
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mutants show severe loss of purple pigmentation and altered anthocyanin composition; no other tradeoff reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful engineering target to increase stable/acylated-xylosylated anthocyanin accumulation when stacked with other anthocyanin pathway genes
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Recognizes 3-O-glucosylated anthocyanidins/flavonols and UDP-xylose specifically; does not accept 3,5-O-diglucosylated anthocyanidins; flavonol 3-O-glucosides are substrates in vitro but corresponding xylosylated flavonols were not detected in seedlings
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ICA and coexpression analysis identified UGT79B1 as an anthocyanin-related candidate gene. 2. ugt79b1 knockout mutants lacked major anthocyanins and total anthocyanin fell to about 33% of wild type under sucrose induction. 3. 35S-driven complementation restored the wild-type anthocyanin profile. 4. Recombinant UGT79B1 converted cyanidin 3-O-glucoside to cyanidin 3-O-xylosyl(1→2)glucoside and used UDP-xylose specifically.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptome ICA; Ds transposon knockout mutant (ugt84a2-1) and known knockout brt1-1/ugt84a2-2; RT-PCR loss of transcript; anthocyanin profiling by HPLC/PDA/ESI-MS; complementation with 35S:UGT84A2; comparison with ugt84a1 mutant; prior biochemical assignment referenced
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 UDP-glycosyltransferase; UGT84 family; PSPG box-containing plant secondary product glycosyltransferase
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Sinapoyl donor supply for anthocyanin acylation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Glucosylates sinapic acid with UDP-glucose to produce 1-O-sinapoylglucose, the acyl donor used for anthocyanin sinapoylation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supplies sinapoylglucose for anthocyanin sinapoylation; knockout drastically reduces major sinapoylated anthocyanin A11 without major change in total anthocyanin
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor/acyl donor pool for target product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Major supplier step for sinapoyl donor to anthocyanin sinapoylation
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Upstream donor-supply step for downstream anthocyanin acylation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Detected in anthocyanin-related ICA subcluster; slight but reproducible induction by PAP1 overexpression reported
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Growth on 12% sucrose-containing medium to induce anthocyanin accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray independent component analysis using 1388 Affymetrix ATH1 arrays from ATTED-II/AtGenExpress
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Anthocyanin sinapoyltransferase SAT (At2g23000) functionally uses its product 1-O-sinapoylglucose
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Alters anthocyanin composition strongly; previously reported slight reduction of sinapoylmalate and altered other sinapoylated/feruloylated compounds
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to enhance specific sinapoylated anthocyanins by increasing sinapoyl donor supply; may require stacking with downstream acyltransferases
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: UGT84A2 has predominant role over UGT84A1 in supplying 1-O-sinapoylglucose for anthocyanin modification, though other UGT84As contribute weakly
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT84A2 was identified by ICA as an anthocyanin-related candidate despite weak simple coexpression with anthocyanin genes. 2. ugt84a2 mutants showed strong reduction of the major sinapoylated anthocyanin A11 to about 25% of wild type, with A5 accumulating instead. 3. 35S-driven complementation restored the wild-type anthocyanin profile. 4. Comparison with ugt84a1 demonstrated that UGT84A2 is the predominant supplier of 1-O-sinapoylglucose for anthocyanin modification.
